svg.aria-label: One log, two views. On the left, the Part 9 event journal for run run-bb11: an append-only list of JSONL events. seq 0 run_started with goal refund ORD-3300. seq 1 llm_decided search_policy with in 40 out 12 and 180 milliseconds. seq 2 tool_result for search_policy, 60 milliseconds. seq 3 llm_decided process_refund with in 70 out 15 and 210 milliseconds. seq 4 tool_result for process_refund, 90 milliseconds. seq 5 llm_decided finish with in 95 out 20 and 150 milliseconds. seq 6 finished. A fold arrow in the middle reads the SAME BYTES and folds them into the OTel-GenAI-shaped span tree on the right, with no otel SDK. The span tree has one root span invoke_agent from 0 to 690 milliseconds carrying gen_ai.operation.name invoke_agent, gen_ai.usage.input_tokens 205, output_tokens 47, and gen_ai.cost_usd 0.00252. Under it, five sibling child spans: an llm chat span from 0 to 180 deciding search_policy, an execute_tool span for search_policy from 180 to 240, an llm chat span from 240 to 450 deciding process_refund, an execute_tool span for process_refund from 450 to 540, and an llm chat span from 540 to 690 deciding finish. The llm spans carry gen_ai.request.model deterministic-rule-controller and token usage. The point is that the durability journal and the observability span tree are the same bytes read two ways.
svg.title: One log, two views: the Part 9 journal folded into an OTel-GenAI-shaped span tree, same bytes, no otel SDK

svg.text: Run run-bb11. Observability is not a second system. The Part 9 event journal, read as spans, is the OTel-GenAI-shaped trace. Same bytes, no otel SDK.

svg.text: Every llm_decided event records its token usage and latency;

text.font-family: Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif

svg.text: every tool_result records its latency. That is everything a span

text.font-family: Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif

svg.text: needs. No extra instrumentation was added for observability.

text.font-family: Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif

svg.text: Note the input tokens GROWING: 40 then 70 then 95. The agent

text.font-family: Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif

svg.text: re-sends a longer transcript every step. That growth, not the

text.font-family: Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif

svg.text: tool calls, is the dominant cost lever.

svg.text: How the fold maps events to spans

text.font-family: Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif

svg.text: Each llm_decided becomes an llm (chat) span; each tool_result becomes

text.font-family: Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif

svg.text: an execute_tool span. All five are SIBLINGS under the single root

text.font-family: Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif

svg.text: invoke_agent span. start_ms and end_ms come straight from the recorded

text.font-family: Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif

svg.text: latencies. Attribute names are hedged as gen_ai.*-STYLE: the GenAI

text.font-family: Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif
Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif

svg.text: The spans are plain dicts emitted as JSONL. A real OTLP exporter would

text.font-family: Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif

svg.text: ship them: a commented one-liner, the same swap-in pattern as generate().

text.font-family: Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif

svg.text: The durability artifact and the observability artifact are the SAME BYTES read two ways. The journal that lets the agent survive a crash, read as spans, makes it debuggable and accountable.
